issues.summary: Upgrade Subscription Renewal Reminder Task to .NET 10
issues.description:
Upgraded the Subscription Renewal Reminder Task to .NET 10 and modernized the application startup, configuration, and dependency management patterns.

Changes include:

- Migrated the project to SDK-style .NET 10

- Replaced App.config with appsettings.json

- Added support for runtime secret substitution and user secrets

- Introduced Host.CreateDefaultBuilder and dependency injection

- Configured WCF connected service clients through code instead of configuration files

- Added centralized application logging abstraction and improved console logging behavior

- Updated service registrations and application configuration handling

- Removed legacy packages folder usage and aligned the project with modern PackageReference conventions

- Cleaned up project structure and modernized startup/configuration patterns
issues.descriptionHtml:
Upgraded the Subscription Renewal Reminder Task to .NET 10 and modernized the application startup, configuration, and dependency management patterns.

Changes include:

  • Migrated the project to SDK-style .NET 10

  • Replaced App.config with appsettings.json

  • Added support for runtime secret substitution and user secrets

  • Introduced Host.CreateDefaultBuilder and dependency injection

  • Configured WCF connected service clients through code instead of configuration files

  • Added centralized application logging abstraction and improved console logging behavior

  • Updated service registrations and application configuration handling

  • Removed legacy packages folder usage and aligned the project with modern PackageReference conventions

  • Cleaned up project structure and modernized startup/configuration patterns

issues.summary: Code Changes

parent.summary: Upgrade Subscription Renewal Reminder Task to .NET 10

issues.summary: Update GolfPass Terms for Employee Comp Email Template
issues.description:
We need to change T&Cs language for the GolfPass+ confirmation email for the employee/complimentary products

Current Copy:

Request

Change the language for renewal T&Cs at the bottom of the email for Cancellation Policy ONLY for the logic listed below:

Your GolfPass+ Employee Complimentary subscription will not auto-renew. You may cancel your complimentary membership at any time, if you choose. Employees must redeem complimentary subscriptions manually for the next period of service through the Employee Perks portal. You can view your benefits on the Manage Memberships page on GolfPass.com.

By subscribing, you agree to our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy.

Logic:

- Use the above T&Cs copy for PPIDs that have ProductPricingTags = IsComplimentary AND IsEmployee

issues.summary: [Commerce] Facility Closure / Update casing Tee Times to Fail in the Add to Cart process
issues.description:
When adding Tee Times to cart when the course has an update (such as reducing to 9 holes from 18 holes) it fails with an error message stating the Tee Time could not be found. Root cause is that the store procedure that gets the tee times only filters facilities if they have an active closure. This needs to be updated so it also looks at if Disable

Inventory is true and allow facilities that are active but don’t have disable inventory set to true.
issues.descriptionHtml:
When adding Tee Times to cart when the course has an update (such as reducing to 9 holes from 18 holes) it fails with an error message stating the Tee Time could not be found. Root cause is that the store procedure that gets the tee times only filters facilities if they have an active closure. This needs to be updated so it also looks at if Disable

Inventory is true and allow facilities that are active but don’t have disable inventory set to true.

issues.summary: CLONE - Update to allow No Payment Methods when $0 due now for BE
issues.description:
As a user of the Golf BE, I shouldn’t have to provide a CC in the legacy flow when I don’t have to pay anything up front in some scenarios. There are many legacy courses with this setup.

We need to update the Process Payment workflow to allow Booking Engine users to not send a payment method for the following:

- Due Now = $0

- Due Later > $0

Today, in this scenario this isn’t possible since there is a due later amount, we are still validating the payment methods. As none are being sent, the following validation error is shown:

Request:

curl -X POST \
-H "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..Rv1IxIReuvcHGwguZAaV5tumZQ66xD1jNX25xl9epIQ" \
-H "com-x-client-token: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJHb2xmTm93UUEiLCJjaGFubmVsSWQiOiIyMDk3MiIsIm5vbmNlIjoiYWMxY2ZjNWZjZmY4NzBiYTRmYjU1ODYyOWQ5MDUzZDhhZDk5YzA4OTYyY2RjOGJkZjBmYjU1N2FmZGFjZDBlYSIsImlhdCI6MTc3NTA3MzY5MiwiZXhwIjoxNzc1MDc1NDkyLCJhdWQiOiJnb2xmbm93LWNvbW1lcmNlIiwiaXNzIjoidW5pZmllZC1jb21tZXJjZS1wbGF0Zm9ybSJ9.Ld5VnDDkrIeR0n5zLGV3_rg_3JhimZTtto_Bn_E0AGc" \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{"originalInvoiceAmount":0,"nonce":"3d5ef7e3-9bb4-4c07-841e-3aef1932fead","authenticatedTenant":{"tenantTypeCode":"booking-engine-identity","identifier":"732e6239-a33a-4ae6-a6a9-64637e6e782d","criteria":{"access_token":"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzaG9wcGluZ0NhcnRJZCI6IjczMmU2MjM5LWEzM2EtNGFlNi1hNmE5LTY0NjM3ZTZlNzgyZCIsImVtYWlsIjoiaGFubmFoNzZ0OEBtYWlsaW5hdG9yLmNvbSIsImlzR3Vlc3QiOmZhbHNlLCJpc0FjY291bnRWZXJpZmllZCI6dHJ1ZSwiaWF0IjoxNzc1MDczNjkyLCJleHAiOjE3NzUwNzcyOTJ9.lNW4kT0Xtzdn9hYWw4QISZugyfQ98yVsk8-wFcrK61s"}},"guest":{"firstName":"Hannah","lastName":"McDuck","emailAddress":"hannah76t8@mailinator.com","phoneNumber":"+12345678900"}}' \
"https://commerce-api.dev.us-east-2.photondash.dev/api/my/v1/payment/process/69cd18224405eb69e43eaeeb"

Response:

{"errorCode":"UNKNOWN_ERROR","errorId":"b06bc93d-d5d6-4b37-9d6c-471e909590a9","errorMessages":"The selected payment methods are invalid: The selected payment methods are not valid for order (store:GN Facility 17266 Store)","level":"error","message":"Commerce API error:","status":400,"url":"my/v1/payment/process/69cd18224405eb69e43eaeeb"}

{"errorCode":"UNKNOWN_ERROR","initialError":{"data":{"CorrelationId":"","Error":{"ID":10012,"Type":"DataValidation"},"ErrorID":"10012","ErrorMessages":["The selected payment methods are invalid: The selected payment methods are not valid for order (store:GN Facility 17266 Store)"],"ErrorType":"DataValidation","ValidationErrors":{}},"message":"Request failed with status code 400","request":{"headers":{},"method":"post","url":"my/v1/payment/process/69cd18224405eb69e43eaeeb"},"status":400},"level":"error","message":"Error occurred: Unknown error occurred. Error Track ID: b06bc93d-d5d6-4b37-9d6c-471e909590a9","name":"AppError","stack":"AppError: Unknown error occurred. Error Track ID: b06bc93d-d5d6-4b37-9d6c-471e909590a9\n    at mapCommerceError (/application/src/services/commerce-api/commerce-error-mapper.ts:69:12)\n    at CommerceApiClient.processPayment (/application/src/api-clients/commerce-api-client.ts:486:35)\n    at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:103:5)\n    at async processPayment (/application/src/services/invoice/process-payment.ts:151:41)\n    at async /application/src/routes/shopping-cart-v2.ts:936:34"}

- In the process payment workflow update the validation to check if any products in the cart requires the credit card to be collect. If there are any then throw the validation error if a payment method is not provided else allow the $0 order to proceed.

- Verify that Authorization and Capture Activites will be bypassed when criteria is met.

Thread: https://versant-golfnow.slack.com/archives/C09MHR4RJ3F/p1775138883976139?thread_ts=1771354328.177829&cid=C09MHR4RJ3F

issues.summary: Upgrade vulnerable and outdated NuGet package(s) identified by Snyk in Spark Task (golf-task-spark)
issues.description:
A Snyk alert identified one or more vulnerable and/or outdated NuGet package dependencies in the Spark Task repository (`golf-task-spark`).

This work includes:

- Reviewing impacted and outdated package version(s)

- Upgrading vulnerable and outdated NuGet package(s) to supported versions

- Validating build and runtime compatibility

- Performing regression testing as needed
issues.descriptionHtml:
A Snyk alert identified one or more vulnerable and/or outdated NuGet package dependencies in the Spark Task repository (`golf-task-spark`).

This work includes:

  • Reviewing impacted and outdated package version(s)

  • Upgrading vulnerable and outdated NuGet package(s) to supported versions

  • Validating build and runtime compatibility

  • Performing regression testing as needed

issues.summary: Follow up from CORE-14094: Bug found in payment during execute fullfillment
issues.description:
Bug found during the payment step when testing CORE-14094

This bug was found during processing payment for a hot deal.  During payment process commerce sends the following request to the execute fulfillment endpoint for the fulfillment  proxy. The endpoint is https://apiv2.tn000.stg.golfnowone.dev/commerce/fulfillment/execute  Here is the request body sent to the proxy

The context for this bug is after we corrected the product code used for the hot deals there have been issues with the metadata that is being passed from commerce to the proxies.  The first issue was the flag for sending to the point of sale tee sheet.  Now we are getting past that step; but have the issue with the execute fulfillment step which is later in the process payment workflow.
issues.descriptionHtml:
Bug found during the payment step when testing CORE-14094

This bug was found during processing payment for a hot deal. During payment process commerce sends the following request to the execute fulfillment endpoint for the fulfillment proxy. The endpoint is https://apiv2.tn000.stg.golfnowone.dev/commerce/fulfillment/execute Here is the request body sent to the proxy


The context for this bug is after we corrected the product code used for the hot deals there have been issues with the metadata that is being passed from commerce to the proxies. The first issue was the flag for sending to the point of sale tee sheet. Now we are getting past that step; but have the issue with the execute fulfillment step which is later in the process payment workflow.

issues.summary: Mass Cancellation -  Include Trade Toggle Not Working as Intended
issues.description:
The new GNC Mass Cancellation Tool has two issues around facility selection and trade inclusion behavior.

First, the page currently allows mass cancellation behavior while a Master Facility / Trade Facility is selected in the facility dropdown. Mass cancellations should only be available from the play. If a Master Facility is selected, the page should prevent searching/cancelling and display a clear message that mass cancellations are not available for Master Facilities.

Second, when a Play Facility is selected, the Include Trade toggle is not working as expected. Enabling the toggle removes the course rounds from the results and also fails to show the trade rounds. The expected behavior is that the toggle expands the results, not replaces or clears them.

Steps to Reproduce

- Open the GNC Mass Cancellation Tool created under CORE-13954.

- Select a Master Facility / Trade Facility in the facility dropdown.

- Observe that the page still allows the mass cancellation workflow to continue.

- Select a Play Facility that has course rounds in the selected date range.

- Search with Include Trade disabled.

- Confirm course rounds appear.

- Enable Include Trade.

- Search again.

- Observe that course rounds disappear and trade rounds are not shown.

Actual Result

- Master Facilities can be selected and used on the Mass Cancellation page.

- When Include Trade is enabled from a Play Facility, the results no longer show the course rounds.

- Trade rounds are also not returned, leaving the user with missing or incorrect results.

Expected Result

- If a Master Facility / Trade Facility is selected, the Mass Cancellation page should not allow the workflow to continue.

- The UI should show this message:

Mass Cancellations are not available for Master Facilities.

- When a Play Facility is selected:

- Include Trade disabled: show only course rounds within the selected date range.

- Include Trade enabled: show both course rounds and trade rounds within the selected date range together.

Acceptance Criteria

- When the selected facility is a Master Facility / Trade Facility, the page displays:
Mass Cancellations are not available for Master Facilities.

- When a Master Facility / Trade Facility is selected, search and cancellation actions are disabled or otherwise blocked.

- When a Play Facility is selected and Include Trade is disabled, only that facility’s course rounds in the selected date range are returned.

- When a Play Facility is selected and Include Trade is enabled, both course rounds and trade rounds in the selected date range are returned in the same results table.

- Enabling Include Trade must not remove course rounds from the result set.

- If no trade rounds exist for the selected Play Facility/date range, enabling Include Trade should still show the course rounds.

- Results should remain limited to the selected date range in both toggle states.
issues.descriptionHtml:
The new GNC Mass Cancellation Tool has two issues around facility selection and trade inclusion behavior.

First, the page currently allows mass cancellation behavior while a Master Facility / Trade Facility is selected in the facility dropdown. Mass cancellations should only be available from the play. If a Master Facility is selected, the page should prevent searching/cancelling and display a clear message that mass cancellations are not available for Master Facilities.

Second, when a Play Facility is selected, the Include Trade toggle is not working as expected. Enabling the toggle removes the course rounds from the results and also fails to show the trade rounds. The expected behavior is that the toggle expands the results, not replaces or clears them.

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Open the GNC Mass Cancellation Tool created under CORE-13954.

  2. Select a Master Facility / Trade Facility in the facility dropdown.

  3. Observe that the page still allows the mass cancellation workflow to continue.

  4. Select a Play Facility that has course rounds in the selected date range.

  5. Search with Include Trade disabled.

  6. Confirm course rounds appear.

  7. Enable Include Trade.

  8. Search again.

  9. Observe that course rounds disappear and trade rounds are not shown.

Actual Result

  • Master Facilities can be selected and used on the Mass Cancellation page.

  • When Include Trade is enabled from a Play Facility, the results no longer show the course rounds.

  • Trade rounds are also not returned, leaving the user with missing or incorrect results.

Expected Result

  • If a Master Facility / Trade Facility is selected, the Mass Cancellation page should not allow the workflow to continue.

  • The UI should show this message:

Mass Cancellations are not available for Master Facilities.

  • When a Play Facility is selected:

    • Include Trade disabled: show only course rounds within the selected date range.

    • Include Trade enabled: show both course rounds and trade rounds within the selected date range together.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When the selected facility is a Master Facility / Trade Facility, the page displays:
    Mass Cancellations are not available for Master Facilities.

  2. When a Master Facility / Trade Facility is selected, search and cancellation actions are disabled or otherwise blocked.

  3. When a Play Facility is selected and Include Trade is disabled, only that facility’s course rounds in the selected date range are returned.

  4. When a Play Facility is selected and Include Trade is enabled, both course rounds and trade rounds in the selected date range are returned in the same results table.

  5. Enabling Include Trade must not remove course rounds from the result set.

  6. If no trade rounds exist for the selected Play Facility/date range, enabling Include Trade should still show the course rounds.

  7. Results should remain limited to the selected date range in both toggle states.

parent.summary: Customer Care - Mass Cancellation

issues.summary: Home Course Selection/Management
issues.description:
We need to create the ability to assign a Home Course for a user and manage the restrictions around Home Course changes.

Requirements

- One customer can have a configurable amount of GolfFacilityIds assigned as Home Course

- Create a platform configuration for maximum number of Home Courses Allowed

- Configuration should be set to 1 for launch but we should support >1 in the future where a customer could potentially have more than 1

- Do not allow selection of Master Facilities

- Create an exclusion list of GolfFacilityIds just in case certain courses refuse to participate

- If a user selects a course on this list, throw an error saying it is not applicable for Home Course selection

- In Prod, this should be empty but we should test by adding some in Dev

- Log the CreatedTimeStamp, LastModifiedTimestamp and LastModifiedBy for selection and modifications

- LastModifiedBy should be the CustomerNumber or CustomerID or SystemUserId if it was done by a service member through GNC

- Create a history log of the changes made for each customer

- Home Course Selection/Modifications

- Any Full Customer without a membership and customers without a product with BenefitID ## (Created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14428)

- You are allowed two selections/modifications in a 1 year period

- One initial selection would start the 1 year clock

- One additional modification within that 1 year period

- Purchasing a product with BenefitId ##

- This will restart the 1 year period with the start date matching the subscription start date

- This new period will exclude any previous modifications and restart at 2

- Multiple purchases of a product within the 1 year period will not affect the date range

- Example: The initial purchase of a monthly product will start the 1 year period but the renewal of the monthly product will not restart at the end of 30 days, it will still be the original 1 year period. After the 1 year period of 12 renewed monthly memberships, you will then start a new 1 year

- As a user that has already selected their home course and they either purchase an applicable product, renew their applicable product or are a non member and have crossed the 365 day mark

- Your previous selection will remain as is and roll over to the new period as a new Home Course record

- You are allowed two modifications with the new 365 day period

- Monthly product renewals do not reset the modification count or 1 year period

- Guest customers should not be able to select a Home Course

- GNC should have the ability to override the Home Course selection at any time for support purposes

- GNC Modifications should be logged in Change Tracking

- GNC Modifications should not count towards the two allowed modification count

- If you have two stacked memberships with one starting in the future, you should not be able to make modifications to the future dated membership Home Course until the subscription starts

- Future dated memberships are now out of scope since all users get a 365 day period no matter what

- There should be an optional reason for choosing a home course

- Reasons:

- Convenience/location

- Price/value

- Course conditions

- Social/community

- Course amenities

- Personal history/familiarity

- There should be an optional reason for modifying home course

- Reasons:

- Course closed

- Course conditions deteriorated

- I moved

- Snow bird

- Accidental selection

- Course no longer fits my wants/needs

Deliverables

- An API for frontend to set a Home Course

- Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

- An API for frontend to modify a Home Course selection

- Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

- Error messaging around modification limit restrictions

- You have reached your Home Course modification limit. Please reach out to GolfNow Support for further assistance.

- Error messaging around selecting the same facility twice when modifying

- [FacilityName] is already selected as your Home Course.

- Can GetCustomer and GetCustomerSubscription APIs also return the Home Course GolfFacilityId? This is a nice to have

- A GNC UI for managing/Supporting Home Course - https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14437
issues.descriptionHtml:
We need to create the ability to assign a Home Course for a user and manage the restrictions around Home Course changes.

Requirements

  • One customer can have a configurable amount of GolfFacilityIds assigned as Home Course

    • Create a platform configuration for maximum number of Home Courses Allowed

      • Configuration should be set to 1 for launch but we should support >1 in the future where a customer could potentially have more than 1

  • Do not allow selection of Master Facilities

  • Create an exclusion list of GolfFacilityIds just in case certain courses refuse to participate

    • If a user selects a course on this list, throw an error saying it is not applicable for Home Course selection

    • In Prod, this should be empty but we should test by adding some in Dev

  • Log the CreatedTimeStamp, LastModifiedTimestamp and LastModifiedBy for selection and modifications

    • LastModifiedBy should be the CustomerNumber or CustomerID or SystemUserId if it was done by a service member through GNC

    • Create a history log of the changes made for each customer

  • Home Course Selection/Modifications

    • Any Full Customer without a membership and customers without a product with BenefitID ## (Created in https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14428)

      • You are allowed two selections/modifications in a 1 year period

        • One initial selection would start the 1 year clock

        • One additional modification within that 1 year period

    • Purchasing a product with BenefitId ##

      • This will restart the 1 year period with the start date matching the subscription start date

      • This new period will exclude any previous modifications and restart at 2

      • Multiple purchases of a product within the 1 year period will not affect the date range

        • Example: The initial purchase of a monthly product will start the 1 year period but the renewal of the monthly product will not restart at the end of 30 days, it will still be the original 1 year period. After the 1 year period of 12 renewed monthly memberships, you will then start a new 1 year

    • As a user that has already selected their home course and they either purchase an applicable product, renew their applicable product or are a non member and have crossed the 365 day mark

      • Your previous selection will remain as is and roll over to the new period as a new Home Course record

      • You are allowed two modifications with the new 365 day period

      • Monthly product renewals do not reset the modification count or 1 year period

    • Guest customers should not be able to select a Home Course

    • GNC should have the ability to override the Home Course selection at any time for support purposes

      • GNC Modifications should be logged in Change Tracking

      • GNC Modifications should not count towards the two allowed modification count

    • If you have two stacked memberships with one starting in the future, you should not be able to make modifications to the future dated membership Home Course until the subscription starts

      • Future dated memberships are now out of scope since all users get a 365 day period no matter what

  • There should be an optional reason for choosing a home course

    • Reasons:

      • Convenience/location

      • Price/value

      • Course conditions

      • Social/community

      • Course amenities

      • Personal history/familiarity

    • There should be an optional reason for modifying home course

      • Reasons:

        • Course closed

        • Course conditions deteriorated

        • I moved

        • Snow bird

        • Accidental selection

        • Course no longer fits my wants/needs

Deliverables

  • An API for frontend to set a Home Course

    • Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

  • An API for frontend to modify a Home Course selection

    • Needs ChannelId in the request so we can store where the Home Course was set/modified

  • Error messaging around modification limit restrictions

    • You have reached your Home Course modification limit. Please reach out to GolfNow Support for further assistance.

  • Error messaging around selecting the same facility twice when modifying

    • [FacilityName] is already selected as your Home Course.

  • Can GetCustomer and GetCustomerSubscription APIs also return the Home Course GolfFacilityId? This is a nice to have

  • A GNC UI for managing/Supporting Home Course - https://golfnow.atlassian.net/browse/CORE-14437

parent.summary: GolfPass "Home Course": Phase I

issues.summary: FeeConfiguration - Create Columns for GP Waivability and Refundability
issues.description:
Goal

Add explicit FeeConfiguration support for GolfPass waived-fee-credit waivability and refundability so each configured fee can independently define whether it can be waived by a GolfPass waived fee credit and whether it is refundable during cancellation/refund flows.

This is a foundational data/model ticket for the Customizable Transaction Fees epic. It should not attempt to build the full multi-fee framework, GNC UI revamp, fee messaging changes, tax handling, or revenue allocation behavior.

---
Background

The Customizable Transaction Fees epic requires different fee types to behave differently. For example:

- The standard GolfNow transaction/convenience fee may be waivable with a GolfPass waived fee credit.

- A tourism, city, county, or other regulatory fee may need to be non-waivable.

- Some fees may be refundable.

- Some fees may be non-refundable.

Today, FeeConfiguration does not have clear per-fee fields that let downstream systems determine these behaviors from configuration. We need to add those fields first so future tickets can safely apply waiver and refund behavior without hard-coding fee categories.

---
Scope

Add new fields to the FeeConfiguration data model to support per-fee behavior:

- GolfPass waivable flag

- Indicates whether this fee can be waived by a GolfPass waived-fee-credit benefit.

- Example values: true for standard GolfNow transaction fee, false for tourism/city/county fees.

- Refundable flag

- Indicates whether this fee is refundable when a reservation is cancelled/refunded.

- Example values: true for refundable convenience fees, false for non-refundable tourism/city/county fees.

Implementation should include the appropriate database/schema changes and any required data access/model/DTO updates so these values can be read by downstream API, Reservation Service, and future GNC configuration work.

---
Defaults / Backward Compatibility

Existing production behavior must remain unchanged after the columns are added.

Default values for existing FeeConfiguration records should preserve current behavior (current behavior is that all fees and waivable and refundable).

---
Acceptance Criteria

- FeeConfiguration schema supports a per-fee GolfPass waivable field.

- FeeConfiguration schema supports a per-fee refundable field.

- Existing FeeConfiguration records are backfilled/defaulted so current production behavior is preserved.

- The new fields can be read wherever FeeConfiguration data is retrieved for API/Reservation Service use.

- No customer-facing behavior changes unless explicitly required to preserve existing behavior.

- No GNC UI changes are included in this ticket.

- Engineering documents the chosen default values for existing fee records.
issues.descriptionHtml:
Goal

Add explicit FeeConfiguration support for GolfPass waived-fee-credit waivability and refundability so each configured fee can independently define whether it can be waived by a GolfPass waived fee credit and whether it is refundable during cancellation/refund flows.

This is a foundational data/model ticket for the Customizable Transaction Fees epic. It should not attempt to build the full multi-fee framework, GNC UI revamp, fee messaging changes, tax handling, or revenue allocation behavior.

Background

The Customizable Transaction Fees epic requires different fee types to behave differently. For example:

  • The standard GolfNow transaction/convenience fee may be waivable with a GolfPass waived fee credit.

  • A tourism, city, county, or other regulatory fee may need to be non-waivable.

  • Some fees may be refundable.

  • Some fees may be non-refundable.

Today, FeeConfiguration does not have clear per-fee fields that let downstream systems determine these behaviors from configuration. We need to add those fields first so future tickets can safely apply waiver and refund behavior without hard-coding fee categories.

Scope

Add new fields to the FeeConfiguration data model to support per-fee behavior:

  • GolfPass waivable flag

    • Indicates whether this fee can be waived by a GolfPass waived-fee-credit benefit.

    • Example values: true for standard GolfNow transaction fee, false for tourism/city/county fees.

  • Refundable flag

    • Indicates whether this fee is refundable when a reservation is cancelled/refunded.

    • Example values: true for refundable convenience fees, false for non-refundable tourism/city/county fees.

Implementation should include the appropriate database/schema changes and any required data access/model/DTO updates so these values can be read by downstream API, Reservation Service, and future GNC configuration work.

Defaults / Backward Compatibility

Existing production behavior must remain unchanged after the columns are added.

Default values for existing FeeConfiguration records should preserve current behavior (current behavior is that all fees and waivable and refundable).

Acceptance Criteria

  1. FeeConfiguration schema supports a per-fee GolfPass waivable field.

  2. FeeConfiguration schema supports a per-fee refundable field.

  3. Existing FeeConfiguration records are backfilled/defaulted so current production behavior is preserved.

  4. The new fields can be read wherever FeeConfiguration data is retrieved for API/Reservation Service use.

  5. No customer-facing behavior changes unless explicitly required to preserve existing behavior.

  6. No GNC UI changes are included in this ticket.

  7. Engineering documents the chosen default values for existing fee records.

issues.description:
Request from @Marcus Briggs to investigate these locations on our Geosearch dataset.

ahmeek, mi
alto, mi
analomink, pa

barto, pa

brimley, mi

etters, pa

falls, pa
finleyville, pa
fleetville, pa
foxburg, pa
gagetown, mi
glennie, mi
gladwyne, pa
goodells, mi
guys-mills, pa
harborcreek, pa
havertown, pa
hessel, mi
hersey, mi
hookstown, pa
imler, pa
kawkawlin, mi
kossuth, pa
kresgeville, pa
kunkletown, pa
landenberg, pa
lanse, mi
linfield, pa
lupton, mi
mc-connellsburg, pa
mc-kean, pa
mc-clure, pa
mc-veytown, pa
mears, mi
melvin, mi
mesick, mi
new-era, mi
newtown-square, pa

Finding

These location are FeatureCode : “PPL”, which are populated places but with population lower then 500 to show on the https://download.geonames.org/export/dump/cities500.zip, where we ingest and mark the location as searchable.

There are around 4516634 records of this FeatureCode type that current aren’t showing up in the search result.

Examples:

Resolution

- Migrate these locations with CountryCode : “US”, FeatureClass : 80, FeatureCode : “PPL”, and IsPostal : false into production database.

- Set fields to IsCity : true and Searchable : true.

issues.summary: New Booking API – Pass is_hotdeal Value in correct location
issues.description:
Description:
The is_hotdeal flag in the New Booking API request is not functioning as expected for EZRTS courses using trade rate sets.

This issue is caused by CORE sending the is_hotdeal value in an incorrect location within the request payload. As a result, the ETN REST API does not recognize the flag, and GolfNow Hot Deal pricing logic is not applied correctly.

Expected Behavior (ETN REST API Contract):
The ETN /v1/tee-time/book endpoint expects is_hotdeal to be nested under the golfnow object within each slot:

"slots": [
{
"golfnow": {
"is_hotdeal": true
}
}
]

Actual Behavior (CORE Request):
CORE is currently sending is_hotdeal outside of the expected golfnow object, resulting in the value being ignored by ETN.

Impact:

-  Hot Deal tee times are not processed correctly

-  Incorrect green fee logic applied for GolfNow Hot Deals

-  Affects EZRTS courses using the New Booking API with trade rate sets

---
Reference API Contract:
ETN Booking Endpoint: /v1/tee-time/book
Swagger: https://api.ezlinksgolf.com/swagger/index.html

{
"sponsor_id": 0,
"course_id": 0,
"tee_time_date_time": "2026-04-16T20:16:52.822Z",
"reservation_type_id": 0,
"slots": [
{
"player": {
"slot_name": "string",
"contact_id": 0,
"g1_customer_id": "string",
"common_id": "string"
},
"green_fee": {
"fee_id": 0,
"fee_amount": 0,
"prepaid_amount": 0,
"cancel_amount": 0
},
"golfnow": {
"is_hotdeal": true
}
}
],
"reservation_note": "string",
"payment": {
"token": "string",
"card_type": "string",
"expiration": "string",
"last4": "string",
"charge_on_book": true
},
"promo_code": "string",
"distribution_amount_to_course": 0,
"distribution_amount_to_golfnow": 0,
"distribution_percentage_to_golfnow": 0
}

---
Acceptance Criteria:

- Request Structure Correction

- is_hotdeal must be passed under slots[].golfnow.is_hotdeal in the New Booking API request.

- Functional Validation

-  When is_hotdeal = true, ETN correctly applies GolfNow Hot Deal green fee logic.

-  When is_hotdeal = false, standard pricing logic is applied.

- Backward Compatibility

-  No impact to non–Hot Deal bookings or existing booking flows.

- Testing Coverage

-  Validate behavior for:

-  Hot Deal tee times (true)

-  Non–Hot Deal tee times (false)

QA TEST: Test on Dubsdread
tee sheet should receive isHotDeal: true

our api returns ishotdeal: false and IsHidden: true
issues.descriptionHtml:
Description:
The is_hotdeal flag in the New Booking API request is not functioning as expected for EZRTS courses using trade rate sets.

This issue is caused by CORE sending the is_hotdeal value in an incorrect location within the request payload. As a result, the ETN REST API does not recognize the flag, and GolfNow Hot Deal pricing logic is not applied correctly.

Expected Behavior (ETN REST API Contract):
The ETN /v1/tee-time/book endpoint expects is_hotdeal to be nested under the golfnow object within each slot:

"slots": [
  {
    "golfnow": {
      "is_hotdeal": true
    }
  }
]

Actual Behavior (CORE Request):
CORE is currently sending is_hotdeal outside of the expected golfnow object, resulting in the value being ignored by ETN.

Impact:

  • Hot Deal tee times are not processed correctly

  • Incorrect green fee logic applied for GolfNow Hot Deals

  • Affects EZRTS courses using the New Booking API with trade rate sets

Reference API Contract:
ETN Booking Endpoint: /v1/tee-time/book
Swagger: https://api.ezlinksgolf.com/swagger/index.html

{
  "sponsor_id": 0,
  "course_id": 0,
  "tee_time_date_time": "2026-04-16T20:16:52.822Z",
  "reservation_type_id": 0,
  "slots": [
    {
      "player": {
        "slot_name": "string",
        "contact_id": 0,
        "g1_customer_id": "string",
        "common_id": "string"
      },
      "green_fee": {
        "fee_id": 0,
        "fee_amount": 0,
        "prepaid_amount": 0,
        "cancel_amount": 0
      },
      "golfnow": {
        "is_hotdeal": true
      }
    }
  ],
  "reservation_note": "string",
  "payment": {
    "token": "string",
    "card_type": "string",
    "expiration": "string",
    "last4": "string",
    "charge_on_book": true
  },
  "promo_code": "string",
  "distribution_amount_to_course": 0,
  "distribution_amount_to_golfnow": 0,
  "distribution_percentage_to_golfnow": 0
}

Acceptance Criteria:

  1. Request Structure Correction

    • is_hotdeal must be passed under slots[].golfnow.is_hotdeal in the New Booking API request.

  2. Functional Validation

    • When is_hotdeal = true, ETN correctly applies GolfNow Hot Deal green fee logic.

    • When is_hotdeal = false, standard pricing logic is applied.

  3. Backward Compatibility

    • No impact to non–Hot Deal bookings or existing booking flows.

  4. Testing Coverage

    • Validate behavior for:

      • Hot Deal tee times (true)

      • Non–Hot Deal tee times (false)

QA TEST: Test on Dubsdread
tee sheet should receive isHotDeal: true

our api returns ishotdeal: false and IsHidden: true

issues.summary: Add two new fields to /get-eligible-promo-codes API
issues.description:
- API: https://api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/5151/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/get-eligible-promo-codes?teeTimeRateId=67973919&playerCount=1

- Current Response

[
{
"Code": "yfz2zMbj7",
"ExpirationDate": "/Date(1805295695000+0000)/",
"IsPromotedCampaign": false,
"ShortDescription": "$10 Monthly Tee Time Credit",
"StackableType": 2
},
{
"Code": "yfz2zMaa6",
"ExpirationDate": "/Date(1805295695000+0000)/",
"IsPromotedCampaign": false,
"ShortDescription": "$10 Monthly Tee Time Credit",
"StackableType": 2
}
]

- We would like to add two new fields to each object in the array

- CouponId- This is the PromotionId from GNC

- PromoTypeId - This is the Promotion Type from GNC

- This can be NULL

- Expected Response:

[
{
"Code": "yfz2zMbj7",
"ExpirationDate": "/Date(1805295695000+0000)/",
"IsPromotedCampaign": false,
"ShortDescription": "$10 Monthly Tee Time Credit",
"StackableType": 2,
"CouponId": 6375,
"PromoTypeId": 1
},
{
"Code": "yfz2zMaa6",
"ExpirationDate": "/Date(1805295695000+0000)/",
"IsPromotedCampaign": false,
"ShortDescription": "$10 Monthly Tee Time Credit",
"StackableType": 2,
"CouponId": 6375,
"PromoTypeId": 1
}
]

- These two fields are already returned on https://api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/loyalty/promoCodes?promoTypeIds=1&activeOnly=true, we just need them to also be returned on the endpoint listed on #1 above

- For reference, these are the two GNC fields where that is controlled
issues.descriptionHtml:
1. API: https://api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/channel/5151/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/get-eligible-promo-codes?teeTimeRateId=67973919&playerCount=1

    1. Current Response

      [
    {
        "Code": "yfz2zMbj7",
        "ExpirationDate": "/Date(1805295695000+0000)/",
        "IsPromotedCampaign": false,
        "ShortDescription": "$10 Monthly Tee Time Credit",
        "StackableType": 2
    },
        {
        "Code": "yfz2zMaa6",
        "ExpirationDate": "/Date(1805295695000+0000)/",
        "IsPromotedCampaign": false,
        "ShortDescription": "$10 Monthly Tee Time Credit",
        "StackableType": 2
    }
]

  2. We would like to add two new fields to each object in the array

    1. CouponId- This is the PromotionId from GNC

    2. PromoTypeId - This is the Promotion Type from GNC

      1. This can be NULL

    3. Expected Response:

      [
    {
        "Code": "yfz2zMbj7",
        "ExpirationDate": "/Date(1805295695000+0000)/",
        "IsPromotedCampaign": false,
        "ShortDescription": "$10 Monthly Tee Time Credit",
        "StackableType": 2,
        "CouponId": 6375,
        "PromoTypeId": 1
    },
        {
        "Code": "yfz2zMaa6",
        "ExpirationDate": "/Date(1805295695000+0000)/",
        "IsPromotedCampaign": false,
        "ShortDescription": "$10 Monthly Tee Time Credit",
        "StackableType": 2,
        "CouponId": 6375,
        "PromoTypeId": 1
    }
]

    4. These two fields are already returned on https://api.gnsvc.com/GolfNowAPI.svc/rest/customers/{{EmailAddress}}/loyalty/promoCodes?promoTypeIds=1&activeOnly=true, we just need them to also be returned on the endpoint listed on #1 above

    5. For reference, these are the two GNC fields where that is controlled

A Snyk alert identified one or more vulnerable and/or outdated NuGet package dependencies in the Logging Service repository (golf-svc-logging).

This work includes:

- Reviewing impacted and outdated package version(s)

- Upgrading vulnerable and outdated NuGet package(s) to supported versions

- Validating build and runtime compatibility

- Performing regression testing as needed

issues.summary: Latency issue with Product.CustomerSubscriptionList stored procedure.
Modify New EZL API workflow to Account for Partial Prepaid to GN - 2
issues.description:
Currently, we are have an issue whenever a tee time is partially paid online with revenue due to GN, we send this over in the API and its not included in the due online to the course so it gets added to the due at course amount. At checkin, the golfer is being charged the amount they already paid to us.

NOTE: This work was already completed in CORE-13898 but was rolled back due to a downstream cart fee issue. We need to look into this issue and find out why touched cart fees for this and also how we can update this without introducing the same issue.

Green Fee vs Prepaid requirements:

- “green_fee”:  due at course amount

- ”prepaid_amount” - should omit $ due to GN and be only prepaid due to golf course

- “green_fee” should also omit $ due to GN and should only show due at course amount

---
Whenever the revenue allocation source goes to GolfNow, we should be sure to omit this from being sent through API

},
"green_fee": {
"fee_id": 0,
"fee_amount": 0,
"prepaid_amount": 0,   -- This should omit $ due to GN
"cancel_amount": 0
}

NOTE: For New EZL API

QA: Please be sure to connect with the RTS QA team to coordinate this testing effort
issues.descriptionHtml:
Currently, we are have an issue whenever a tee time is partially paid online with revenue due to GN, we send this over in the API and its not included in the due online to the course so it gets added to the due at course amount. At checkin, the golfer is being charged the amount they already paid to us.

NOTE: This work was already completed in CORE-13898 but was rolled back due to a downstream cart fee issue. We need to look into this issue and find out why touched cart fees for this and also how we can update this without introducing the same issue.

Green Fee vs Prepaid requirements:

  • “green_fee”: due at course amount

  • ”prepaid_amount” - should omit $ due to GN and be only prepaid due to golf course

  • “green_fee” should also omit $ due to GN and should only show due at course amount

Whenever the revenue allocation source goes to GolfNow, we should be sure to omit this from being sent through API

},   
 "green_fee": {   
 "fee_id": 0,   
 "fee_amount": 0,   
 "prepaid_amount": 0,   -- This should omit $ due to GN
 "cancel_amount": 0   
 }

NOTE: For New EZL API

QA: Please be sure to connect with the RTS QA team to coordinate this testing effort

});

added.summary: Upgrade Subscription Renewal Reminder Task to .NET 10
added.description:
Upgraded the Subscription Renewal Reminder Task to .NET 10 and modernized the application startup, configuration, and dependency management patterns.

Changes include:

- Migrated the project to SDK-style .NET 10

- Replaced App.config with appsettings.json

- Added support for runtime secret substitution and user secrets

- Introduced Host.CreateDefaultBuilder and dependency injection

- Configured WCF connected service clients through code instead of configuration files

- Added centralized application logging abstraction and improved console logging behavior

- Updated service registrations and application configuration handling

- Removed legacy packages folder usage and aligned the project with modern PackageReference conventions

- Cleaned up project structure and modernized startup/configuration patterns
added.descriptionHtml:
Upgraded the Subscription Renewal Reminder Task to .NET 10 and modernized the application startup, configuration, and dependency management patterns.

Changes include:

  • Migrated the project to SDK-style .NET 10

  • Replaced App.config with appsettings.json

  • Added support for runtime secret substitution and user secrets

  • Introduced Host.CreateDefaultBuilder and dependency injection

  • Configured WCF connected service clients through code instead of configuration files

  • Added centralized application logging abstraction and improved console logging behavior

  • Updated service registrations and application configuration handling

  • Removed legacy packages folder usage and aligned the project with modern PackageReference conventions

  • Cleaned up project structure and modernized startup/configuration patterns
